questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: If $y=\int_{u(x)}^{v(x)} f(t) d t$, let us define $\frac{d y}{d x}$ in a different manner as $\frac{d y}{d x}=v^{\prime}(x) f^2(v(x))-u^{\prime}(x) f^2(u(x))$ and the equation of the tangent at $(a, b)$ as $y-b=\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)_{(a, b)}(x-a)$Question: If $y=\int_x^{x^2} t^2 d t$, then equation of tangent at $x=1$ is
questions.solution:
At $x=1, y=0$
$\frac{d y}{d x}=2 x \cdot\left(x^4\right)^2-\left(x^2\right)^2=2-1=1$
$\therefore$ Equation of tangent $\mathrm{y}-0=1(\mathrm{x}-1), \mathrm{y}=\mathrm{x}-1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following write up carefully and answer the following questions: If $f(x)$ is a differentiable function wherever it is continuous and $f^{\prime}\left(c_1\right)=f^{\prime}\left(c_2\right)=0$, $f^{\prime \prime}\left(c_1\right) f^{\prime \prime}\left(c_2\right) \lt 0, f\left(c_1\right)=5, f\left(c_2\right)=0$ and $\left(c_1 \lt c_2\right)$Question: If $f(x)$ is continuous in $\left[c_1, c_2\right]$ and $f^{\prime \prime}\left(c_1\right)-f^{\prime \prime}\left(c_2\right)\gt0$, then minimum number of roots of $f(x)$ $=0$ in $\left[c_1-1, c_2+1\right]$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & f^{\prime \prime}\left(c_1\right)\gtf^{\prime \prime}\left(c_2\right) \\ & f^{\prime \prime}\left(c_1\right)\gt0 \text { and } f^{\prime \prime}\left(c_2\right) \lt 0 \\ & \Rightarrow \text { at } x=c_1, f \text { has local minimum and at } x=c_2, f \text { has local maximum }\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: If $f: R \rightarrow R$ and $f(x)=g(x)+h(x)$ where $g(x)$ is a polynomial and $h(x)$ is a continuous and differentiable bounded function on both sides, then $f(x)$ is onto if $g(x)$ is of odd degree and $f(x)$ is into if $g(x)$ is of even degree. To check whether $f(x)$ is one-one we need to differentiate $f(x)$. If $f^{\prime}(x)$ changes sign in domain of $f$ then f is many-one else one-oneQuestion:$f: R \rightarrow R$ and $f(x)=a_1 x+a_3 x^3+a_5 x^5+\ldots+a_{2 n+1} x^{2 n+1}-\cot ^{-1} x$ where $0 \lt a_1 \lt a_3 \lt \ldots \lt a_{2 n+1}$ then the function $f(x)$ is
questions.solution: $f(x)=$ odd degree polynomial + bounded function $\cot ^{-1} x \in(0, \pi)$ also, $f^{\prime}(x)\gt0$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following write up carefully and answer the following questions: If $f(x)$ is a differentiable function wherever it is continuous and $f^{\prime}\left(c_1\right)=f^{\prime}\left(c_2\right)=0$, $f^{\prime \prime}\left(c_1\right) f^{\prime \prime}\left(c_2\right) \lt 0, f\left(c_1\right)=5, f\left(c_2\right)=0$ and $\left(c_1 \lt c_2\right)$Question: If $f(x)$ is continuous in $\left[c_1, c_2\right]$ and $f^{\prime \prime}\left(c_1\right)-f^{\prime \prime}\left(c_2\right)\gt0$, then minimum number of roots of $f^{\prime}(x)$ $=0$ in $\left[c_1-1, c_2+1\right]$ is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{f}^{\prime \prime}\left(\mathrm{c}_1\right)\gt\mathrm{f}^{\prime \prime}\left(\mathrm{c}_2\right) \\
& \mathrm{f}^{\prime}\left(\mathrm{c}_1\right)\gt0 \text { and } \mathrm{f}^{\prime \prime}\left(\mathrm{c}_2\right) \lt 0
\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow$ at $x=c_1, f$ has local minimum and at $x=c_2, f$ has local maximum
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: If $y=\int_{u(x)}^{v(x)} f(t) d t$, let us define $\frac{d y}{d x}$ in a different manner as $\frac{d y}{d x}=v^{\prime}(x) f^2(v(x))-u^{\prime}(x) f^2(u(x))$ and the equation of the tangent at $(a, b)$ as $y-b=\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)_{(a, b)}(x-a)$Question: If $F(x)=\int^x e^{t^2 / 2}\left(1-t^2\right) d t$, then $\frac{d}{d x} F(x)$ at $x=1$ is
questions.solution:
$F(x)=\int_1^x e^{t^2 / 2}\left(1-t^2\right) d t$
$F^{\prime}(x)=\left(e^{\frac{x^2}{2}}\left(1-x^2\right)\right)^2$
$\therefore F^{\prime}(1)=e^{1 / 2} \cdot 0=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $C$ be the curve $f(x)=(\ln x)^2+2 \ln x$ and $A(a, f(a)), B(b, f(b))$ where $(a \lt b)$ are the points of tangency of two tangents drawn from origin to the curve CQuestion:The value of the product ab is equal to
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& y=f(x)=(\ln x)^2+2 \ln x \\
& \left.\frac{d y}{d x}\right|_{A(a, f(a))}=\frac{2 \ln a}{a}+\frac{2}{a}=\frac{2}{a}(1+\ln a)
\end{aligned}$
Also $\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{f(a)-0}{a-0}$
$\frac{f(a)}{a}=\frac{2}{a}(1+\ln a) \Rightarrow f(a)=2(1+\ln a)$
So $(\ln a)^2+2 \ln a=2+2 \ln \mathrm{a}$
$(\ln a)^2=2 \Rightarrow a=e^{\sqrt{2}}$ or $e^{-\sqrt{2}}$
Hence $a=e^{-\sqrt{2}}$ and $b=e^{\sqrt{2}}$
So $a b=1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

To check whether $f(x)$ is one-one we need to differentiate $f(x)$. If $f^{\prime}(x)$ changes sign in domain of $f$ then f is many-one else one-oneQuestion:$f: R \rightarrow R$ and $f(x)=\frac{x\left(x^4+1\right)(x+1)+x^4+2}{x^2+x+1}$, then $f(x)$ is

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f: R \rightarrow R, y=f(x), f(0)=0, \Gamma(x)\gt0$ and $f^{\prime}(x)\gt0$. Three points $A(\alpha, f(\alpha)), B(\beta, f(\beta)), C(y, f(y))$ on $y=f(x)$ such that $0 \lt \alpha \lt \beta \lt \gamma$Question: Which of the following is false
questions.solution: Consider $\mathrm{g}(\mathrm{x})=\frac{\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x})}{\mathrm{x}}$ $g^{\prime}(x)=\frac{f^{\prime}(x) x-f(x)}{x^2}=\frac{f^{\prime}(x)}{x^2}\left(x-\frac{f(x)}{f^{\prime}(x)}\right)$ as $\frac{f^{\prime}(x)}{x^2}\gt0$ Let $h(x)=x-\frac{f(x)}{f^{\prime}(x)}$ $h^{\prime}(x)=1-\frac{f^{\prime}(x)^2-f(x) f^{\prime \prime}(x)}{f^{\prime}(x)^2}=\frac{f(x) f^{\prime \prime}(x)}{f^{\prime}(x)^2}\gt0$ $\therefore x-\frac{f(x)}{f^{\prime}(x)}$ is increasing function $\begin{aligned} & x-\frac{f(x)}{f^{\prime}(x)}\gt0-\frac{f(0)}{f^{\prime}(0)}=0 \\ & \therefore g^{\prime}(x)\gt0 \\ & \therefore \frac{f(y)}{y}\gt\frac{f(\beta)}{\beta}\gt\frac{f(\alpha)}{\alpha}\end{aligned}$ Satisfies A, C. D
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f: R \rightarrow R, y=f(x), f(0)=0, \Gamma(x) \gt 0$ and $f^{\prime}(x) \gt 0$. Three points $A(\alpha, f(\alpha)), B(\beta, f(\beta)), C(y, f(y))$ on $y=f(x)$ such that $0 \lt \alpha \lt \beta \lt \gamma$ Question: Which of following is true
questions.solution: As the function is concave up $\frac{f(\alpha)+f(\beta)}{2} \gt f\left(\frac{\alpha+\beta}{2}\right)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f: R \rightarrow R, y=f(x), f(0)=0, \Gamma(x)\gt0$ and $f^{\prime}(x)\gt0$. Three points $A(\alpha, f(\alpha)), B(\beta, f(\beta)), C(y, f(y))$ on $y=f(x)$ such that $0 \lt \alpha \lt \beta \lt \gamma$Question: Which of the following is true
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { As } \gamma+\beta-\alpha\gt\gamma \\ & \frac{f(\gamma+\beta-\alpha)}{\gamma+\beta-\alpha}\gt\frac{f(\gamma)}{\gamma}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $x^4+4 x^3-8 x^2+p=0$ be an equationQuestion: Values of $p$ when all roots are real
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & x^4+4 x^3-8 x^2+p=0 \\ & f(x)=4 x^3+12 x^2-16 x=0 \\ & x=0,-4,1\end{aligned}$ $\lim _{x \rightarrow \infty} f(x)=\infty=\lim _{x \rightarrow-\infty} f(x)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: A line PQ parallel to the diagonal BD of a square $A B C D$ with side length ' $a$ ' unit is drawn at a distance $x$ from the verter $A$, where $x \in[0, \sqrt{2}$ a| cuts the adjacent sides at $P$ and $Q$. Let $f(x)$ be the area of the segment of a square cut off by PQ. with A as one of the vertex.Question: Let $g(x)=f^{-1}(x)$, then the domain of $g(x)$ is
questions.solution: Two cases are possible for PQ w.r.L diagonal OA Case -1 : When $x=A R \leq$ AS i.e. $x \in\left[0, \frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}\right]$ Triangle ARP is an isosceles right angle triangle, Hence $A P=\sqrt{2} x=A Q$ $\Rightarrow$ Area of segment i.e. $f(x)=\frac{1}{2}(\sqrt{2} x)^2=x^2, x \in\left[0, \frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}\right]$ Case -II: When $x=\mathrm{AR} \geq \mathrm{AS}$ i.e. $x \in\left[\frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}, \sqrt{2} a\right]$ $\Rightarrow C P=C Q=\sqrt{2}(\sqrt{2} a-x)$ Area of segment $f(x)=a^2-(\sqrt{2} a-x)^2$ $\begin{aligned} & x \in\left[\frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}, \sqrt{2} a\right] \\ & \text { i.c. f: }[0, \sqrt{2} a] \rightarrow\left|0, a^2\right|\end{aligned}$ $f(x)= \begin{cases}x^2, & 0 \leq x \leq \frac{a}{\sqrt{2}} \\ a^2-(\sqrt{2} a-x)^2, & \frac{a}{\sqrt{2}} \leq x \leq a\end{cases}$ When $0 \leq x \leq \frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}, f(x)=x^2, 0 \leq f(x) \leq \frac{a^2}{2}$ Put $x=f^{-1}(x) \Rightarrow\left(f^{-1}(x)\right)^2=x, 0 \leq x \leq \frac{a^2}{2}, 0 \leq f^{-1}(x) \leq \frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}$ $\Rightarrow f^{-1}(x)=\sqrt{x}, 0 \leq x \leq \frac{a^2}{2}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { When } \frac{a}{\sqrt{2}} \leq x \leq \sqrt{2} a, f(x)=a^2-(\sqrt{2} a-x)^2, \frac{a^2}{2} \leq f(x) \leq a^2 \\ & \text { Put } x=f^{-1}(x), x=a^2-\left(\sqrt{2} a-f^{-1}(x)\right)^2, \frac{a^2}{2} \leq x \leq a^2, \frac{a}{\sqrt{2}} \leq f^{-1}(x) \leq \sqrt{2} a\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow\left(\sqrt{2} a-f^{-1}(x)\right)^2=a^2-x \\ & \Rightarrow \sqrt{2} a-f^{-1}(x)= \pm \sqrt{a^2-x} \\ & \Rightarrow f^{-1}(x)=\sqrt{2} a \mp \sqrt{a^2-x}\end{aligned}$ Neglecting positive sign since $\frac{a}{\sqrt{2}} \leq f^{\prime}(x) \leq \in \sqrt{2} a$ $\Rightarrow f^{\prime}(x)=\sqrt{2} a-\sqrt{a^2-x}$ $f^{-1}(x)= \begin{cases}\sqrt{x}, & 0 \leq x \leq \frac{a^2}{2} \\ \sqrt{2} a-\sqrt{a^2-x}, & \frac{a^2}{2} \leq x \leq a^2\end{cases}$ Hence domain of $f^{-1}(x)$ is $x \in\left[0, a^2\right]$ For $\mathrm{a}=2$. $f(x)= \begin{cases}x^2, & 0 \leq x \leq \sqrt{2} \\ 4-(2 \sqrt{2}-x)^2, & \sqrt{2} \leq x \leq 2\end{cases}$ And $f^{-1}(x)= \begin{cases}\sqrt{x}, & 0 \leq x \leq 2 \\ 2 \sqrt{2}-\sqrt{4-x}, & 2 \leq x \leq 4\end{cases}$ Domain of $\sqrt{f^{-1}(x)-f(x)}$ is when $f^{-1}(x)-f(x) \geq 0$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { Case -I: } 0 \leq x \leq \sqrt{2} \\ & \sqrt{x}-x^2 \geq 0 \Rightarrow \sqrt{x}\left(1-x^{3 / 2}\right) \geq 0 \\ & \Rightarrow 0 \leq x \leq 1 \\ & \text { Case }- \text { II: } \sqrt{2} \leq x \leq 2 \\ & f^{-1}(x)=\sqrt{x} \text { i.e. } f^{-1}(x) \in\left[2^{1 / 4} \cdot \sqrt{2}\right]\end{aligned}$ And $f(x)=4-(2 \sqrt{2}-x)^2, f(x) \in[2,8(\sqrt{2}-1)]$ Hence $\mathrm{f}^{-1}(\mathrm{x})-\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x}) \geq 0 \forall \mathrm{x} \in \phi$. Hence domain of $\phi(x)=\sqrt{f^{-1}(x)-f(x)}$ is $x \in[0,1]$ for $a=2$ The equation $f(x)-f^{\prime}(x)=0$ has exactly three solutions $\begin{aligned} & f(x)= \begin{cases}x^2, & 0 \leq x \leq \frac{a}{\sqrt{2}} \\ a^2-(\sqrt{2} a-x)^2, & \frac{a}{\sqrt{2}} \leq x \leq a\end{cases} \\ & f^{-1}(x)= \begin{cases}\sqrt{x}, & 0 \leq x \leq \frac{a^2}{2} \\ \sqrt{2} a-\sqrt{a^2-x}, & \frac{a^2}{2} \leq x \leq a^2\end{cases} \end{aligned}$ Exactly three solutions exist if $\frac{a^2}{2}=\frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}$ i.c. $a=\sqrt{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: A line PQ parallel to the diagonal BD of a square $A B C D$ with side length ' $a$ ' unit is drawn at a distance $x$ from the verter $A$, where $x \in[0, \sqrt{2}$ a| cuts the adjacent sides at $P$ and $Q$. Let $f(x)$ be the area of the segment of a square cut off by PQ. with A as one of the vertex.Question: For $a=2$, the domain of the function $\phi(x)=\sqrt{f^{-1}(x)-f(x)}$ is/are
questions.solution: Two cases are possible for PQ w.r.L diagonal OA Case -1 : When $x=A R \leq$ AS i.e. $x \in\left[0, \frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}\right]$ Triangle ARP is an isosceles right angle triangle, Hence $A P=\sqrt{2} x=A Q$ $\Rightarrow$ Area of segment i.e. $f(x)=\frac{1}{2}(\sqrt{2} x)^2=x^2, x \in\left[0, \frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}\right]$ Case -II: When $x=\mathrm{AR} \geq \mathrm{AS}$ i.e. $x \in\left[\frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}, \sqrt{2} a\right]$ $\Rightarrow C P=C Q=\sqrt{2}(\sqrt{2} a-x)$ Area of segment $f(x)=a^2-(\sqrt{2} a-x)^2$ $\begin{aligned} & x \in\left[\frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}, \sqrt{2} a\right] \\ & \text { i.c. f: }[0, \sqrt{2} a] \rightarrow\left|0, a^2\right|\end{aligned}$ $f(x)= \begin{cases}x^2, & 0 \leq x \leq \frac{a}{\sqrt{2}} \\ a^2-(\sqrt{2} a-x)^2, & \frac{a}{\sqrt{2}} \leq x \leq a\end{cases}$ When $0 \leq x \leq \frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}, f(x)=x^2, 0 \leq f(x) \leq \frac{a^2}{2}$ Put $x=f^{-1}(x) \Rightarrow\left(f^{-1}(x)\right)^2=x, 0 \leq x \leq \frac{a^2}{2}, 0 \leq f^{-1}(x) \leq \frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}$ $\Rightarrow f^{-1}(x)=\sqrt{x}, 0 \leq x \leq \frac{a^2}{2}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { When } \frac{a}{\sqrt{2}} \leq x \leq \sqrt{2} a, f(x)=a^2-(\sqrt{2} a-x)^2, \frac{a^2}{2} \leq f(x) \leq a^2 \\ & \text { Put } x=f^{-1}(x), x=a^2-\left(\sqrt{2} a-f^{-1}(x)\right)^2, \frac{a^2}{2} \leq x \leq a^2, \frac{a}{\sqrt{2}} \leq f^{-1}(x) \leq \sqrt{2} a\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow\left(\sqrt{2} a-f^{-1}(x)\right)^2=a^2-x \\ & \Rightarrow \sqrt{2} a-f^{-1}(x)= \pm \sqrt{a^2-x} \\ & \Rightarrow f^{-1}(x)=\sqrt{2} a \mp \sqrt{a^2-x}\end{aligned}$ Neglecting positive sign since $\frac{a}{\sqrt{2}} \leq f^{\prime}(x) \leq \in \sqrt{2} a$ $\Rightarrow f^{\prime}(x)=\sqrt{2} a-\sqrt{a^2-x}$ $f^{-1}(x)= \begin{cases}\sqrt{x}, & 0 \leq x \leq \frac{a^2}{2} \\ \sqrt{2} a-\sqrt{a^2-x}, & \frac{a^2}{2} \leq x \leq a^2\end{cases}$ Hence domain of $f^{-1}(x)$ is $x \in\left[0, a^2\right]$ For $\mathrm{a}=2$. $f(x)= \begin{cases}x^2, & 0 \leq x \leq \sqrt{2} \\ 4-(2 \sqrt{2}-x)^2, & \sqrt{2} \leq x \leq 2\end{cases}$ And $f^{-1}(x)= \begin{cases}\sqrt{x}, & 0 \leq x \leq 2 \\ 2 \sqrt{2}-\sqrt{4-x}, & 2 \leq x \leq 4\end{cases}$ Domain of $\sqrt{f^{-1}(x)-f(x)}$ is when $f^{-1}(x)-f(x) \geq 0$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { Case -I: } 0 \leq x \leq \sqrt{2} \\ & \sqrt{x}-x^2 \geq 0 \Rightarrow \sqrt{x}\left(1-x^{3 / 2}\right) \geq 0 \\ & \Rightarrow 0 \leq x \leq 1 \\ & \text { Case }- \text { II: } \sqrt{2} \leq x \leq 2 \\ & f^{-1}(x)=\sqrt{x} \text { i.e. } f^{-1}(x) \in\left[2^{1 / 4} \cdot \sqrt{2}\right]\end{aligned}$ And $f(x)=4-(2 \sqrt{2}-x)^2, f(x) \in[2,8(\sqrt{2}-1)]$ Hence $\mathrm{f}^{-1}(\mathrm{x})-\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{x}) \geq 0 \forall \mathrm{x} \in \phi$. Hence domain of $\phi(x)=\sqrt{f^{-1}(x)-f(x)}$ is $x \in[0,1]$ for $a=2$ The equation $f(x)-f^{\prime}(x)=0$ has exactly three solutions $\begin{aligned} & f(x)= \begin{cases}x^2, & 0 \leq x \leq \frac{a}{\sqrt{2}} \\ a^2-(\sqrt{2} a-x)^2, & \frac{a}{\sqrt{2}} \leq x \leq a\end{cases} \\ & f^{-1}(x)= \begin{cases}\sqrt{x}, & 0 \leq x \leq \frac{a^2}{2} \\ \sqrt{2} a-\sqrt{a^2-x}, & \frac{a^2}{2} \leq x \leq a^2\end{cases} \end{aligned}$ Exactly three solutions exist if $\frac{a^2}{2}=\frac{a}{\sqrt{2}}$ i.c. $a=\sqrt{2}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f(\mathrm{x})=\mathrm{x}^3-3 \mathrm{x}+1, \mathrm{x} \in \mathrm{R}$ and $\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}, \mathrm{c}$ are roots of $f(\mathrm{x})=0, \mathrm{P}(\alpha, f(\alpha))$ is point of local minima $\mathrm{Q}(\beta$, $f(\beta))$ is point of local maxima and $\mathrm{R}(\gamma, f(\gamma))$ is point of inflection in the graph of $\mathrm{y}=f(\mathrm{x})$. On the basis of above information answer the following :Question: Triangle PQR is -
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & f^{\prime}=3\left(x^2-1\right)=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad x=1,-1 \\ & \alpha=1, \beta=-1 \\ & f^{\prime \prime}=0\end{aligned}$ $\Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{x}=0$ is point of inflection so $\quad \mathrm{P}(1,-1) \quad \mathrm{Q}(-1,3) \quad \mathrm{R}(0,1)$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { so } \quad \mathrm{P}(1,-1) \quad \mathrm{Q}(-1,3) \quad \mathrm{R}(0,1) \\ & \Delta=\frac{1}{2}\left|\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -1 & 1 \\ -1 & 3 & 1 \\ 0 & 1 & 1\end{array}\right|=\frac{1}{2}[2-1-1]=0 \\ & \text { so } \text { collinear }\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f(\mathrm{x})=\mathrm{x}^3-3 \mathrm{x}+1, \mathrm{x} \in \mathrm{R}$ and $\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}, \mathrm{c}$ are roots of $f(\mathrm{x})=0, \mathrm{P}(\alpha, f(\alpha))$ is point of local minima $\mathrm{Q}(\beta$, $f(\beta))$ is point of local maxima and $\mathrm{R}(\gamma, f(\gamma))$ is point of inflection in the graph of $\mathrm{y}=f(\mathrm{x})$. On the basis of above information answer the following :Question: Number of distinct real solutions of $f(f(\mathrm{x}))=0$ are -
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & f^{\prime}(x)=3\left(x^2-1\right) \\ & f(0)=1 \\ & f(1)=-1 \\ & f(-1)=3 \\ & f(f(x))=0\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow f^3(\mathrm{x})-3 f(\mathrm{x})+1=0 \Rightarrow f(\mathrm{x})=\alpha, \beta, \gamma \\ & f(\mathrm{x})=\alpha\end{aligned}$ $\Rightarrow$ exactly one solution as $\alpha \in(-1,-2)$ $f(x)=\beta$ $\Rightarrow$ exactly three distinct solutions as $\beta \in(0,1)$ $f(\mathrm{x})=\gamma$ $\Rightarrow$ exactly three distinct solutions as $\gamma \in(1,2)$ so total seven solutions.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $y=f(x)$ be a differentiable function passing through $(1,0)$. Let slope of the tangent at the point $(x, f(x))$ be $m_1$ and slope of line joining the point and origin be $m_2$. Also $\left|\frac{\log \left(m_1+m_2\right)}{\log x}\right|=\frac{2}{1}$. If $f_1(x)$ and $f_2(x)$ are 2 function satisfying the above property where $f_1(x)$ is an algebraic function and $f_2(x)$ is a transcendental function.Question: The value of $\int_{-1}^1\left(f_1(x)+\frac{1}{4 x}\right) d x$ is equal to:
questions.solution:
$\begin{array}{cl}\text { Case-I : } & \frac{\log \left(m_1+m_2\right)}{\log x}=2 \\ \therefore & m_1+m_2=x^2 \\ & f^{\prime}(x)+\frac{f(x)}{x}=x^2\end{array}$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { I.F. }=e^{\ln x}=x \\ & x f(x)=\frac{x^4}{4}+C \\ & \text { At }(1,0) \quad \Rightarrow \quad C=\frac{-1}{4} \\ & f_1(x)=\frac{x^3}{4}-\frac{1}{4 x} ...(1)\\ & \text { Case-II : } \frac{\log \left(m_1+m_2\right)}{\log x}=-2 \\ & \therefore \quad m_1+m_2=\frac{1}{x^2} \\ & f^{\prime}(x)+\frac{f(x)}{x}=\frac{1}{x^2} \\ & \text { I.F. }=x \\ & \end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & x f(x)=\ln x+C \\ \text { At }(1,0) \quad \Rightarrow \quad C & =0 \\ & f_2(x)=\frac{\ln x}{x}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $y=f(x)$ be a differentiable function passing through $(1,0)$. Let slope of the tangent at the point $(x, f(x))$ be $m_1$ and slope of line joining the point and origin be $m_2$. Also $\left|\frac{\log \left(m_1+m_2\right)}{\log x}\right|=\frac{2}{1}$. If $f_1(x)$ and $f_2(x)$ are 2 function satisfying the above property where $f_1(x)$ is an algebraic function and $f_2(x)$ is a transcendental function.Question: Which one of the following statement is correct?
options: $f_1(x)$ is an increasing function $\forall x \in R$.
questions.solution:
$\begin{array}{cl}\text { Case-I : } & \frac{\log \left(m_1+m_2\right)}{\log x}=2 \\ \therefore & m_1+m_2=x^2 \\ & f^{\prime}(x)+\frac{f(x)}{x}=x^2\end{array}$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { I.F. }=e^{\ln x}=x \\ & x f(x)=\frac{x^4}{4}+C \\ & \text { At }(1,0) \quad \Rightarrow \quad C=\frac{-1}{4} \\ & f_1(x)=\frac{x^3}{4}-\frac{1}{4 x} ...(1)\\ & \text { Case-II : } \frac{\log \left(m_1+m_2\right)}{\log x}=-2 \\ & \therefore \quad m_1+m_2=\frac{1}{x^2} \\ & f^{\prime}(x)+\frac{f(x)}{x}=\frac{1}{x^2} \\ & \text { I.F. }=x \\ & \end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & x f(x)=\ln x+C \\ \text { At }(1,0) \quad \Rightarrow \quad C & =0 \\ & f_2(x)=\frac{\ln x}{x}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider the function defined implicitly $y^2+y-x=2$ on various intervals on the real line. If $\mathrm{y} \in\left(-\infty,-\frac{1}{2}\right)$, the equation implicitly defines a unique real valued differentiable function $\mathrm{y}=f(\mathrm{x})$. On the basis of above informations, answer the following questions.Question: The value of $f$ "(4) is -
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \because \quad y^2+y-x=2 \quad \Rightarrow \quad 2 y \frac{d y}{d x}+\frac{d y}{d x}=1 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1}{2 y+1}\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{1}{2 y+1}$
put $\quad x=4 \Rightarrow y=2,-3$
$\begin{aligned} & y \in\left(-\infty,-\frac{1}{2}\right) \Rightarrow y=-3 \\ & \left.\Rightarrow \quad \frac{\mathrm{dy}}{\mathrm{dx}}\right|_{x=4}=\left.\frac{1}{2 y+1}\right|_{y=-3}=-\frac{1}{5}\end{aligned}$
Now $(1+2 y) \frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}+2\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)^2=0$
$\left.\Rightarrow \quad \frac{\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{dx}^2}\right|_{\mathrm{x}=4}=\frac{2}{125} \quad \Rightarrow \quad f^{\prime \prime}(4)=\frac{2}{125}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider the function defined implicitly $y^2+y-x=2$ on various intervals on the real line. If $\mathrm{y} \in\left(-\infty,-\frac{1}{2}\right)$, the equation implicitly defines a unique real valued differentiable function $\mathrm{y}=f(\mathrm{x})$. On the basis of above informations, answer the following questions.Question: If $\mathrm{y}=\mathrm{h}(\mathrm{x})$ is mirror image of $\mathrm{y}=f(\mathrm{x})$ about the line $2 \mathrm{y}+1=0$, then $f^{\prime \prime}\left(\mathrm{x}_0\right)+\mathrm{h}^{\prime \prime}\left(\mathrm{x}_0\right)$, where $\mathrm{x}_0 \in$ $\left(-\frac{9}{4}, \infty\right)$ is -
questions.solution:
$\because \quad \mathrm{h}(\mathrm{x})$ is mirror image of $f(\mathrm{x})$ in $\mathrm{y}=-\frac{1}{2}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{f(\mathrm{x})+\mathrm{h}(\mathrm{x})}{2}=-\frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow f(\mathrm{x})+\mathrm{h}(\mathrm{x})=-1 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad f^{\prime \prime}(\mathrm{x})+\mathrm{h}^{\prime \prime}(\mathrm{x})=0 \quad \forall \mathrm{x} \in\left(-\frac{9}{4}, \infty\right)\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Graph of $y=P(x)=a x^5+b x^4+c x^3+d x^2+e x+f$, is given
Question: If $P^{\prime \prime}(x)=0$ has real roots $\alpha, \beta, \gamma$ then $[\alpha]+[\beta]+[\gamma]$ is equal to: [Note: Where [] denotes greatest integer function]
questions.solution:
Here $P^{\prime}(x)=0$ at $x=-2,-1,0, \frac{1}{2}$
$\therefore \quad P^{\prime \prime}(x)=0$ will have roots $\alpha, \beta, \gamma$ as $\alpha \in(-2,-1), \beta \in(-1,0)$ and $\gamma \in\left(0, \frac{1}{2}\right)$
$$
\therefore \quad[\alpha]+[\beta]+[\gamma]=-2-1+0=-3
$$
and equation $P^{\prime}(x) P^{\prime \prime}(x)=0$ will have seven real roots as $P^{\prime}(x)=0$ has four roots and $P^{\prime \prime}(x)=0$ has three roots.
$\therefore \quad$ Its derivative $\left(P^{\prime \prime}(x)\right)^2+P^{\prime}(x) \cdot P^{\prime \prime \prime}(x)=0$ will have 6 real roots.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q:
Paragraph: Graph of $y=P(x)=a x^5+b x^4+c x^3+d x^2+e x+f$, is given
Question: The minimum number of real roots of the equation $\left(P^{\prime \prime}(x)\right)^2+P^{\prime}(x) \cdot P^{\prime \prime \prime}(x)=0$ is:
questions.solution:
Here $P^{\prime}(x)=0$ at $x=-2,-1,0, \frac{1}{2}$
$\therefore \quad P^{\prime \prime}(x)=0$ will have roots $\alpha, \beta, \gamma$ as $\alpha \in(-2,-1), \beta \in(-1,0)$ and $\gamma \in\left(0, \frac{1}{2}\right)$
$$
\therefore \quad[\alpha]+[\beta]+[\gamma]=-2-1+0=-3
$$
and equation $P^{\prime}(x) P^{\prime \prime}(x)=0$ will have seven real roots as $P^{\prime}(x)=0$ has four roots and $P^{\prime \prime}(x)=0$ has three roots.
$\therefore \quad$ Its derivative $\left(P^{\prime \prime}(x)\right)^2+P^{\prime}(x) \cdot P^{\prime \prime \prime}(x)=0$ will have 6 real roots.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Consider an open cylindrical reservoir whose cross-sectional view is as shown in the figure. The thickness of the wall of cylinder is 1 cm as shown in the figure \& the volume of the reservoir is $27 \pi \mathrm{~cm}^3$. Let $x ~\&~ h$ be the radius and the height of the cylinder and $v(x)=\pi\left\{(x+a)^2+\frac{b}{x}+\frac{c}{x^2}\right\}$, where $v(x)$ represents the volume of the material required to construct the cylinder expressed as a function of radius ' $x$ ' of the cylinder. On the basis of above informations, answer the following questions :
Question: If the material cost to construct the cylinder is minimum, then which of the following relations must hold between $x$ \& h -
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & v^{\prime}(x)=\pi\left\{2(x+1)-\frac{54}{x^2}-\frac{54}{x^3}\right\} \\ & =2 \pi\left\{\frac{x^4+x^3-27 x-27}{x^3}\right\}=2 \pi\left\{\frac{x^3(x+1)-27(x+1)}{x^3}\right\} \\ & =2 \pi\left\{\frac{(x+1)(x-3)\left(x^2+3 x+9\right)}{x^3}\right\}\end{aligned}$
at $x=3$ function has global minima at $\mathrm{x}=3, \mathrm{~h}=3$ from equation (i)
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

Paragraph: Consider an open cylindrical reservoir whose cross-sectional view is as shown in the figure. The thickness of the wall of cylinder is 1 cm as shown in the figure \& the volume of the reservoir is $27 \pi \mathrm{~cm}^3$. Let $x \& h$ be the radius and the height of the cylinder and $v(x)=\pi\left\{(x+a)^2+\frac{b}{x}+\frac{c}{x^2}\right\}$, where $v(x)$ represents the volume of the material required to construct the cylinder expressed as a function of radius ' $x$ ' of the cylinder. On the basis of above informations, answer the following questions :
Question: If the function $v(x)$ is redefined such that $v: R_0 \rightarrow R$, then number of solutions of the equation $\mathrm{v}(\mathrm{x})=0$ are -

On the basis of sign scheme of $v^{\prime}(x)$ in Q .10 graph of $\mathrm{v}(\mathrm{x})$ is as shown below.
from figure it is clear that the equation $v(x)=0$ has 2 roots.
